title: podman pull 403 forbiden with region blocked ip
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

I was having problems pulling a docker image, and the only info I get was:

```
 podman  pull --all-tags --authfile pull-secret.json quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-release:4.9.8-aarch64
Getting image source signatures
Error: reading blob sha256:aeb7866.....b3bf7: Error fetching blob: invalid status code from registry 403 (Forbidden)
```



**Describe the results you received:**

This only happens from my laptop (home network). But I could pull other images from other registries. It is not a real authentication problem, I have checked it well. The same pull with the same auth works in other hosts. So, it was a problem in my netowrk
After several tries I tried the same using docker and I discovered more information about the error:
```
Not continuing with pull after error: error pulling image configuration: denied: The region from which you are pulling has been geo-ip blocked. Please contact the namespace owner. 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Maybe podman would also give more details. Because it is not just a 403 error, it is a problem with my network ip and my ISP. The error from docker command is much more clear and it can help you to solve your connectivity problem.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

comments.body: @vrothberg @mtrmac PTAL I assume this must be fixed in c/image.
Note to self: Looks like `GetBlob` should call `registryHTTPResponseToError`, not just `httpResponseToError`.
@mtrmac how do I test that change?

Maybe this would help. I took the blob image from podman output and tried to download just with Firefox.

```
HTTP Response headers
HTTP/2 403 Forbidden
server: nginx/1.12.1
date: Fri, 26 Nov 2021 15:40:06 GMT
content-type: application/json
content-length: 154
X-Firefox-Spdy: h2
```

```
HTTP Request headers
GET /v2/openshift-release-dev/ocp-release/blobs/sha256:40c264676...f2103aeac4b HTTP/2
Host: quay.io
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Fedora; Linux x86_64; rv:94.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/94.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/avif,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: es-ES,es;q=0.8,en-US;q=0.5,en;q=0.3
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br
DNT: 1
Connection: keep-alive
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1
Sec-Fetch-Dest: document
Sec-Fetch-Mode: navigate
Sec-Fetch-Site: none
Sec-Fetch-User: ?1
Cache-Control: max-age=0
TE: trailers
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I can confirm the real problem was an IP Region Ban. The Docker command info was very precise.

After checking with quay.io team, I have solved the issue of my ip region ban because of an outdated DDBB. But I still think that podman returned error would be more clear. Forbidden 403 make you think on accessing a privated repo, or credentials... But the real problem is a region ban problem. Docker command returns a more clear error, which what it helped me to solve the problem .
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mtrmac was this ever fixed?
See the state of the linked PR.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
we're seeing this on [quay](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/PROJQUAY-3590) as well - let me know if I can help with testing.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

It’s fairly likely that #15662 will fix this, but I don’t have a way to test that.

Can someone who is experiencing this try Podman with that PR, please?

And for our test suite, it would be useful to have a capture of the failing request (headers at least, make sure to drop any credentials) and the failing response (_both_ headers and body).
@flavianmissi are you still seeing the issue?
I'm not longer working closely with the Quay team, but I pinged their QE about this @vrothberg. Will let you know what they find.
That's very kind. Thank you, @flavianmissi !

Got [a reply from QE](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/PROJQUAY-3590?focusedId=22485034&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-22485034)
Looks like they can no longer reproduce the issue.
Thank you!  I think we can close it then :)
